basics.name: Antonio Pedicini
basics.label: Master's Degree Student in Electronics Engineering for Automation and Sensing at Università degli Studi del Sannio, Benevento, Italy
basics.summary: Master’s student in Electronic Engineering specializing in Automation, with a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Engineering. Passionate about embedded systems, IoT, and machine learning. Experienced in programming languages such as C/C++, Python, and Java. Skilled in microcontroller programming, sensor integration, and data analysis. Eager to apply my knowledge and skills to real-world projects and contribute to the field of automation and sensing technology.

courses: Dynamics and Control of Switched Dynamic Systems
Measurements for Automation and Industrial Processes

projects.name: A Data-Driven Testbench for Fault Detection of UAV Propellers
projects.summary: Developed an automated, cost-effective testbench for UAV propeller fault diagnosis using Machine Learning algorithms for a Bachelor's Thesis.
highlights: Managed data acquisition of mechanical vibrations using an Arduino-based system.
Trained and evaluated classification models (SVM, KNN, Decision Trees) using Python and scikit-learn.
Successfully detected unbalanced loads yielding competitive performance.

projects.name: Computational Thermoelectric Generator Model Study & Optimization
projects.summary: Developed a computational model of a polymer-based Thermoelectric Generator (TEG) using COMSOL Multiphysics to evaluate performance under thermal conditions typical of aircraft flight scenarios.
highlights: Coupled Heat Transfer in Solids and Electrical Circuit physics interfaces.
Re-designed the base model into a Segmented TEG (STEG) incorporating Skutterudite.
Applied bound-constrained optimization algorithms to maximize thermal conversion efficiency.

projects.name: Reinforcement Learning for Chess Endgames
projects.summary: Explored Deep Reinforcement Learning to solve the King and Queen versus King endgame, applying a Deep Q-learning (DQN) approach to train an autonomous agent.
highlights: Structured a Curriculum Learning environment to incrementally increase difficulty.
Evaluated and compared FCN and CNN for optimal state evaluation.
Developed the simulation setup using MATLAB and the Reinforcement Learning Toolbox.

projects.name: Bridgeless Boost PFC Converter: Control Strategies Comparison
projects.summary: Evaluated the performance of three distinct control strategies for the Bridgeless Boost PFC Converter (BBC) through averaged and small-signal modeling techniques.
highlights: Implemented and compared the cascaded PI/PR and Lyapunov-based inner control loops.
Utilized the dynamic phasors approach using an SOGI-PLL.
Conducted a comprehensive comparative evaluation using PLECS simulations.

projects.name: Automated Measurement System for Flow Rate Sensor Calibration
projects.summary: Architected a LabVIEW solution utilizing Finite State Automata and Producer-Consumer paradigms to fully automate the calibration procedure for flow rate sensors.
highlights: Developed Arduino firmware to sample measurements from a reference digital flow rate sensor.
Designed the signal conditioning circuit for acquiring the analog Device Under Test (DUT) output.
Automated the generation of prototype Digital Calibration Certificates (DCC) upon procedure completion, significantly streamlining post-test documentation.
